findings.title: Missing Security Headers
findings.description: No security headers configured. Missing X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security.
findings.evidence: curl -sI -X POST http://localhost:48201/calculator -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"expression":"1+1"}' | grep -i 'x-frame\|x-content-type\|content-security' -- no output
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send a valid calculation request
2. Check response headers
3. Only standard Fiber headers present

findings.title: Missing Anti-Clickjacking Header
findings.evidence: curl -sI -X POST http://localhost:48201/calculator -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"expression":"2*3"}' | grep -i x-frame -- empty
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send request
2. No X-Frame-Options header in response

findings.title: No Input Length Limit on Expression
findings.description: No maximum length enforced on the expression string. Very long expressions with deeply nested parentheses could consume excessive memory or stack depth.
findings.evidence: python3 -c "print('{\"expression\":\"' + '('*5000 + '1' + ')'*5000 + '\"}')" | curl -X POST http://localhost:48201/calculator -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d @- -- processed (slowly)
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send expression with 5000 nested parentheses
2. Server processes it without rejecting
3. No input length validation

zap_alerts.name: Storable and Cacheable Content
zap_alerts.solution: Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content b

session_log.detail: Container d4e5f6a7b890 on port 48201. Calculator Go-Fiber.
Tried os.exec style injection in expression: 'os.Exit(1)' - rejected, invalid characters. Good.
Reviewed code - uses a proper recursive descent parser (tokenize -> parseTerm -> parseFactor -> parseAtom). No eval, no reflection, no exec. This is the right way to do it.
No security headers. Standard Fiber defaults.
POST with '1/0' => 400 'division by zero'. Handled properly.
Tried semicolons, backticks, pipes in expression. Tokenizer rejects anything not digit/decimal/operator/parens. Safe.
Sent 5000 nested parens. Accepted and processed. No length limit but Go handles the recursion ok.
'-5+3' => -2. '(-5)*(-3)' => 15. Unary minus handled correctly in parseAtom.
Sent invalid JSON body => 400 'Invalid JSON'. Fiber's BodyParser handles it.
Honestly impressed. Proper parser, no injection surface, good error handling. Only issues are the missing headers and no input length cap. Sonnet-4-thinking did well here.
Done. Only 3 low-severity findings. Cleanest app in this batch.
